reason: Despite a week of positive Wall Street news, Bitcoin failed to sustain gains above $70,000 and experienced rapid profit-taking after a short-lived jump to $74,000. Negative spillovers from traditional finance (BlackRock fund issues) are impacting crypto prices and DeFi markets, indicating a 'risk-off' sentiment. The inability to hold key psychological levels and immediate selling pressure after rallies point to a distribution phase. Ongoing regulatory scrutiny (Binance probe) adds further uncertainty and potential downside risk.
headlines: Why bitcoin couldn't hold $70,000 despite its best week of Wall Street news in months
Managing financial AI agents is the only skill you’ll need to survive the AI layoffs
BlackRock private credit fund is latest to crack, hitting crypto prices and DeFi markets
Bitcoin buyers are cashing out fast after short-lived jump to $74,000
Binance tells Senate probe no accounts sent crypto directly to Iran
